name: sherlock
title: sherlock
description:
Find usernames across social networks
Sherlock relies on the site's designers providing a  unique URL
for a registered username. To determine if a username is available,
Sherlock queries that URL, and uses to response to understand if
there is a claimed username already there.
.
Currently, the tool is capable of locating users on more than 300 social
networks: Apple Developer, Arduino, Docker Hub, GitHub, GitLab,  Facebook,
BitCoinForum, CNET, IFTTT, Instagram, PlayStore  PyPI, Scribd, Telegram,
TikTok, Tinder etc.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use sherlock because Find usernames across social networks.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use sherlock as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sherlock [POSITION] [argument]` as documented upstream for sherlock.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair sherlock with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `sherlock --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
